title: Contact Canadian Government Annuities - Canada.ca
content:
## Contact Canadian Government Annuities
Information about the Canadian Government Annuities Program
You can complete the following tasks online:
- download  the Designation / Change of Beneficiary form
- download the Designation / Revocation of Beneficiary form
You can complete the following tasks by calling:
- request direct deposit or change direct deposit information
- change your mailing address
- get a missing tax slip
- report the death of an annuitant
- get guidance on power of attorney process
You will need your Annuity Contract Number  or your Social Insurance Number.
If you are calling about direct deposit,  you will also need:
- the name and number of your  bank or financial institution
- the number of the branch   where you have your account
- your bank account number
Canada and the United States toll-free : 1-800-561-7922 Hours of operation:  Monday to Friday from 8:30 am to 4:30 pm local time. Closed statutory holidays.
Outside Canada and the United States : 1-506-548-7972 (Long-distance charges apply) Hours of operation:  Monday to Friday from  7 am to 7:30 pm Eastern time. Closed statutory holidays.
You can complete the following tasks by mail:
- designate, revoke or change  annuities beneficiary
- send power of attorney  documents
- request direct deposit or  change direct deposit information
- change your mailing address
- get a missing tax slip
- report the death of an  annuitant
- upon receiving the certificate  of identity you must return it dually completed, along with any other requested  documents
Canadian Government Annuities Branch Service Canada PO Box 12000 Bathurst NB  E2A 4T6
Note: Service Canada Centres are unable to answer your questions about individual or group annuities.
